Remote Work Florida Vacation Homes

Combine work and vacation. Our homes offer reliable internet and workspaces plus pools and beaches.

✍️ By Seascape Vacations Team | Last Updated: 2026-03-02

The rise of remote work has created a new category of traveler: people who can work from anywhere and choose to work from paradise. Florida's Gulf Coast — with its sunshine, beaches, and relaxed pace — is one of the most popular remote work destinations in the country, and our vacation rentals are set up to support productive work alongside an incredible lifestyle.

Seascape Vacations homes come equipped with high-speed WiFi, dedicated workspace-friendly areas, and the kind of quiet residential settings that let you take Zoom calls without background noise. Imagine finishing your workday, closing your laptop, and walking straight out to your private heated pool — or driving 10 minutes to Anna Maria Island for a sunset beach session before dinner.

Extended stays of 2–4+ weeks qualify for significant monthly discounts, making a Gulf Coast work-from-home vacation surprisingly affordable — especially when you consider that you're eliminating commute costs and getting a lifestyle upgrade. Our homes have full kitchens for easy meal prep, laundry facilities, and all the comforts of home. Book direct with Seascape Vacations for the best extended-stay rates.

Common Questions

Frequently Asked Questions

Do your vacation rentals have reliable WiFi for remote work?

Yes, all our properties are equipped with high-speed WiFi suitable for video conferencing, file uploads, and general remote work needs. We regularly test and upgrade our internet connections to ensure reliable performance for guests who need to work during their stay.

Is there a dedicated workspace in the homes?

Our homes have areas suitable for setting up a workspace — dining tables, desk nooks, and quiet rooms that work well for video calls. While they're vacation homes first, many remote workers find our open floor plans provide comfortable and functional work setups.

Do you offer monthly rates for remote workers?

Yes! Extended stays of 2+ weeks qualify for significant discounts over our nightly rates, and monthly stays offer even deeper savings. Contact us for a personalized quote — working from our Florida properties for a month can be surprisingly affordable.

What's the work-life balance like in Bradenton/Sarasota?

It's hard to beat: work during your normal hours, then spend your evenings and weekends on Gulf Coast beaches, at world-class restaurants, kayaking, golfing, or just floating in your private pool. Many remote workers tell us they're more productive here because they start each day refreshed.

Are the neighborhoods quiet enough for work calls?

Our properties are located in quiet residential neighborhoods, not on busy tourist strips. You'll have minimal background noise for video calls and meetings. The private, enclosed pool areas and indoor spaces provide multiple options for quiet work environments.

What time zone is Bradenton/Sarasota in?

Bradenton and Sarasota are in the Eastern Time Zone (ET), the same as New York, Atlanta, and Miami. This makes scheduling easy if you work with East Coast teams, and the 3-hour offset from Pacific Time means you can finish early and still have afternoon sunshine.
